title: Quick start: Eurovision Song Contest country participation
code: d <- ljmds.read.csv("eurovision") dim(d$X)                  # 68 x 52 #> [1] 68 52 range(d$t)                # 1956 2023 #> [1] 1956 2023 head(d$keywords, 10)      # country names #>  [1] "Andorra"            "Albania"            "Armenia"            #>  [4] "Austria"            "Australia"          "Azerbaijan"         #>  [7] "Bosnia_Herzegovina" "Belgium"            "Bulgaria"           #> [10] "Belarus"

title: Quick start: Eurovision Song Contest country participation
text: minimal grid used vignette builds quickly; finer grid recommended practice (e.g., h.grid = c(3, 5, 8, 12, 20)). trivial k = 2 split excluded leaving 2 k.grid (default 3:6).
code: sel <- ljmds.select(d$X, d$t,                     h.grid = c(12, 20),                     k.grid = 4:5) sel$h.hat #> [1] 20 sel$k.hat #> [1] 5 sel$S.hat #> [1] 0.4749629 round(sel$S, 3) #>        k4    k5 #> h12 0.369 0.384 #> h20 0.418 0.475

title: Quick start: US Presidential Inaugural Addresses
text: corpus ships CSV column 1 calendar year columns 2.. 0/1 indicators keyword presence (source text included).
code: d <- ljmds.read.csv("inaugural") dim(d$X)                  # 59 x 106 #> [1]  59 106 range(d$t)                # 1789 2021 #> [1] 1789 2021 head(d$keywords, 10) #>  [1] "people"     "government" "nation"     "country"    "power"      #>  [6] "world"      "citizen"    "time"       "peace"      "man"

title: Quick start: US Presidential Inaugural Addresses

code: sel <- ljmds.select(d$X, d$t,                     h.grid = c(30, 50),                     k.grid = 3:4) sel$h.hat #> [1] 50 sel$k.hat #> [1] 3 sel$S.hat #> [1] 0.3310261 round(sel$S, 3) #>        k3    k4 #> h30 0.261 0.133 #> h50 0.331 0.177

title: Quick start: Peace Declaration of Hiroshima
code: d <- ljmds.read.csv("peace_declaration") dim(d$X)                  # 78 x 95 #> [1] 78 95 range(d$t)                # 1947 2025 #> [1] 1947 2025 head(d$keywords, 10) #>  [1] "weapon"    "Hiroshima" "world"     "peace"     "people"    "year"      #>  [7] "war"       "hibakusha" "bombing"   "city"

title: Quick start: Peace Declaration of Hiroshima

title: Quick start: Peace Declaration of Hiroshima
Quick start: Peace Declaration of Hiroshima
text: Cluster centroid trajectories. Ward dendrogram H. Silhouette heatmap maximizer marker. Time-collapsed MDS map. Class mean occurrence curves. Per-class smoothed curves. pre-rendered animation ships package; locate system.file() view directly:  regenerate GIF fitted object (writes new file current working directory; takes two minutes), uncomment run:
code: plot(fit, type = "trajectory") plot(fit, type = "dendrogram") plot(sel) plot(fit, type = "cmd") plot(fit, type = "means") plot(fit, type = "panels") gif_path <- system.file("extdata", "peace_declaration.gif",                         package = "ljmds") gif_path #> [1] "C:/Users/ksato/AppData/Local/Temp/RtmpOeJTLU/temp_libpath115442fc12dc/ljmds/extdata/peace_declaration.gif" browseURL(gif_path)              # open in default browser # magick::image_read(gif_path)   # or: open in RStudio Viewer # gif <- ljmds.animate(fit, file = "peace_declaration.gif", #                   trail = 10, fps = 2)

text: Kenichi Satoh. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Satoh K (2026). ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS Longitudinal Binary Data. R package version 0.0.1, https://github.com/ksatohds/ljmds.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data},   author = {Kenichi Satoh},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://github.com/ksatohds/ljmds}, }

what: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: R package implementing methodology Satoh (2026): visualisation longitudinal binary data (time × attribute 0/1 matrix) locally weighted Jaccard distance, multidimensional scaling sequential modification (Mizuta, 2003), Ward clustering trajectory distance, functional Rousseeuw silhouette criterion joint selection bandwidth class number.

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: frame plots attributes modified MDS configuration 𝐲j(t)\bm{y}_j(t) calendar year tt, font/symbol size proportional smoothed occurrence fj(t)f_j(t) fading trail class centroids previous frames. Cluster colours follow default Classic Tableau palette silhouette criterion 𝒮(k,h)\mathcal{S}(k, h) jointly selects (k,h)(k, h) user-supplied grid.

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("ksatohds/ljmds", build_vignettes = TRUE)

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
code: library(ljmds)  # Peace Declaration of Hiroshima (built-in) d <- ljmds.read.csv("peace_declaration")   # year + 95 keyword 0/1 columns # d <- ljmds.read.csv("inaugural")          # alternative built-in # d <- ljmds.read.csv(file = "my.csv")      # external CSV  # Joint (h, k) selection over a grid # k = 1, 2 are excluded by leaving them out of k.grid (default 3:6). sel <- ljmds.select(d$X, d$t,                     h.grid = c(3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, 15, 20)) sel$h.hat   # 8 sel$k.hat   # 4 sel$S.hat   # 0.201  # Full pipeline fit <- ljmds.pipeline(d$X, d$t, h = sel$h.hat, k = sel$k.hat)  # Figures (default class.col = grDevices::palette.colors(8, "Classic Tableau")) plot(fit, type = "trajectory")    # centroid trajectories on MDS map plot(fit, type = "dendrogram")    # Ward dendrogram + class boxes plot(fit, type = "cmd")           # time-collapsed MDS of trajectory distance plot(fit, type = "means")         # class mean occurrence curves plot(fit, type = "panels")        # per-class small multiples (square layout) plot(sel)                          # silhouette heatmap with maximizer marker  # GIF animation ljmds.animate(fit, file = "peace_declaration.gif", trail = 10, fps = 2)

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Three longitudinal binary datasets ship inst/extdata: file header row, column 1 named year, columns 2.. giving 0/1 indicators. first two contain source text — derivative summaries.

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Source: scraped City Hiroshima official site (English Peace Declarations) https://www.city.hiroshima.lg.jp/english/. Pre-processing: tokenization, part--speech filtering lemmatization UDPipe (Straka & Straková, 2017) using English Universal Dependencies model english-ewt-ud-2.5-191206.udpipe (CC--SA-NC). Adjacent noun pairs merged compound keywords (e.g. atomic_bomb, nuclear_weapons, United_Nations); words appearing 20+ times whole corpus retained keywords. Year coverage: 78 years (1950 omitted declaration delivered year). Reproducibility scripts: R取得20260504.R (rvest scrape) R前処理20260504.R (UDPipe + quanteda) project data directory; bundled CSV final 0/1 matrix without source text.

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Source: derived data_corpus_inaugural object shipped quanteda R package (Benoit, K. et al., 2018, JOSS 3(30), 774). Pre-processing: UDPipe + quanteda pipeline Peace Declarations; words appearing 50+ times retained keywords; adjacent noun pairs merged (e.g. United_States). Year coverage: 59 inaugural addresses delivered 1789 (Washington) 2021 (Biden), roughly four-year cadence.

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Source: derived Eurovision dataset Spijkervet (2020) Mirovision living-dataset project Burgoyne, Spijkervet & Baker (2023) https://github.com/Amsterdam-Music-Lab/mirovision, released MIT licence Amsterdam Music Lab University Amsterdam. Pre-processing: row contest year, column country, cell 1 country fielded contestant year 0 otherwise. 2020 contest cancelled absent matrix. Year coverage: 68 contest years 1956 (first Eurovision Song Contest) 2023. Spijkervet, J. (2020). Eurovision Dataset (version 1.0). Zenodo. doi:10.5281/zenodo.4036457 Burgoyne, J. ., Spijkervet, J. & Baker, D. J. (2023). Measuring Eurovision Song Contest: living dataset real-world MIR. Proc. 24th International Society Music Information Retrieval Conference (ISMIR), Milan, Italy. https://archives.ismir.net/ismir2023/paper/000097.pdf

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
code: d <- ljmds.read.csv("peace_declaration")  # or "inaugural" / "eurovision" str(d)                       # list(t = numeric n, X = n×p 0/1 matrix,                              #      keywords = column names of X)

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data

(2003) Multidimensional scaling dissimilarity functions continuous argument. Journal Japanese Society Computational Statistics 15(2), 327–333.

title: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: MIT.

what: ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data — ljmds-package
title: ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data — ljmds-package
text: Visualisation longitudinal binary data (time--attribute 0/1 matrix) locally weighted Jaccard distance, multidimensional scaling sequential modification sense Mizuta (2003), Ward clustering trajectory distance, functional Rousseeuw silhouette criterion joint selection bandwidth class number.

title: ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data — ljmds-package
text: ljmds.read.csv() reads (year, keyword1, keyword2, ...) CSV. ljmds.select() performs joint (h, k) selection. ljmds.pipeline() runs full pipeline fixed (h, k). plot.ljmds() reproduces diagnostic figures. ljmds.animate() writes GIF trajectory map.

title: ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data — ljmds-package
text: Two binary corpora ship CSV inst/extdata; see ljmds_data.

title: ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data — ljmds-package
text: Rousseeuw, P.J. (1987) Silhouettes: graphical aid interpretation validation cluster analysis. Journal Computational Applied Mathematics 20, 53–65. Mizuta, M. (2003) Multidimensional scaling dissimilarity functions continuous argument. Journal Japanese Society Computational Statistics 15(2), 327–333.

title: ljmds: Local Jaccard MDS for Longitudinal Binary Data — ljmds-package
text: Maintainer: Kenichi Satoh kenichi-satoh@biwako.shiga-u.ac.jp (ORCID)

what: Animated visualisation of the configuration map — ljmds.animate
title: Animated visualisation of the configuration map — ljmds.animate
text: Renders one PNG frame per observed time point modified MDS configuration: attribute drawn \(\bm{y}_j(t)\) symbol size proportional smoothed occurrence \(f_j(t)\), class colours, fading trail class centroids previous trail frames.

title: Animated visualisation of the configuration map — ljmds.animate
code: ljmds.animate(   x,   file = "ljmds_animation.gif",   trail = 7,   fps = 2,   class.col = grDevices::palette.colors(8, "Classic Tableau"),   frame.dir = NULL )

title: Animated visualisation of the configuration map — ljmds.animate
text: x object returned ljmds.pipeline(). file Output GIF path (used magick available). trail Number trailing frames centroid trail. fps Frames per second GIF. class.col Optional colour palette classes. Defaults grDevices::palette.colors(8, "Classic Tableau"), recycled k exceeds length. frame.dir Optional directory write per-frame PNGs.  NULL (default) magick available, temporary directory used removed GIF written; magick available, fresh directory ljmds_frames created current working directory kept (frames lost).

title: Animated visualisation of the configuration map — ljmds.animate
text: Invisibly, GIF file path magick available, otherwise directory containing per-frame PNGs.

title: Animated visualisation of the configuration map — ljmds.animate
text: Frame generation uses base R graphics, works minimal install.  magick package available per-time PNGs additionally assembled GIF animation; otherwise GIF assembly step skipped (message) frames kept disk user can assemble GIF later another tool.

what: Local Jaccard MDS pipeline at fixed (h, k) — ljmds.pipeline
title: Local Jaccard MDS pipeline at fixed (h, k) — ljmds.pipeline
text: Run full pipeline local Jaccard distance, multi-dimensional scaling sequential Mizuta modification, trajectory distance, Ward clustering.  Returns cluster labels, per-time configurations, trajectory distance, smoothed curves class mean curves.

title: Local Jaccard MDS pipeline at fixed (h, k) — ljmds.pipeline
Local Jaccard MDS pipeline at fixed (h, k) — ljmds.pipeline
text: X Integer/numeric n x p matrix 0/1 keyword occurrences. Rows time points, columns keywords. t Numeric vector length n giving time row. h Bandwidth (standard deviation Gaussian kernel). k Number classes.

title: Local Jaccard MDS pipeline at fixed (h, k) — ljmds.pipeline
text: list class "ljmds": labels: integer p-vector cluster ids, xs, ys: n x p matrices MDS coordinates, H: p x p trajectory distance, f: n x p smoothed occurrence curves, m: n x k class mean curves, t, h, k, keywords.

what: Read a longitudinal binary keyword corpus — ljmds.read.csv
title: Read a longitudinal binary keyword corpus — ljmds.read.csv
text: Reads CSV column 1 time variable (e.g. calendar year) columns 2.. 0/1 indicators attribute presence time point.  Two example corpora ship package can loaded name; external CSV can loaded passing path file.

title: Read a longitudinal binary keyword corpus — ljmds.read.csv
Read a longitudinal binary keyword corpus — ljmds.read.csv
text: name Built-dataset name; one "peace_declaration", "inaugural", "eurovision".  Ignored file supplied.  Defaults "peace_declaration". file Optional path user-supplied CSV.  given, name ignored file read directly.

title: Read a longitudinal binary keyword corpus — ljmds.read.csv
text: list components t (numeric n-vector times), X (n x p binary matrix), keywords (column names X).

title: Read a longitudinal binary keyword corpus — ljmds.read.csv
code: # Built-in Peace Declaration of Hiroshima d <- ljmds.read.csv("peace_declaration") dim(d$X)              # 78 95 #> [1] 78 95  # Built-in US Presidential Inaugural Addresses d <- ljmds.read.csv("inaugural") dim(d$X)              # 59 106 #> [1]  59 106  # Built-in Eurovision Song Contest country participation d <- ljmds.read.csv("eurovision") dim(d$X)              # 68 52 #> [1] 68 52  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # User-supplied CSV (column 1 = year, columns 2.. = 0/1) d <- ljmds.read.csv(file = "my_corpus.csv") } # }

what: Joint (h, k) selection by functional Rousseeuw silhouette — ljmds.select
title: Joint (h, k) selection by functional Rousseeuw silhouette — ljmds.select
text: candidate bandwidth h.grid[] class number k.grid[j], runs ljmds.pipeline() computes functional Rousseeuw silhouette resulting partition.  Returns grid silhouette values maximizer (h, k).

title: Joint (h, k) selection by functional Rousseeuw silhouette — ljmds.select
Joint (h, k) selection by functional Rousseeuw silhouette — ljmds.select
text: X Integer/numeric n x p matrix 0/1 keyword occurrences. t Numeric vector length n. h.grid Numeric vector candidate bandwidths. k.grid Integer vector candidate class numbers; default 3:6 excludes trivial k = 2 split.

title: Joint (h, k) selection by functional Rousseeuw silhouette — ljmds.select
text: list class "ljmds.sel": S: matrix silhouette values, rows = h.grid, cols = k.grid, h.hat, k.hat: maximizer supplied grid, S.hat: silhouette maximizer, h.grid, k.grid.

title: Joint (h, k) selection by functional Rousseeuw silhouette — ljmds.select
text: trivial k = 2 split typically gives silhouette value larger admissible (k >= 3) cell, k = 1 partition .  excluded simply putting k.grid; default k.grid = 3:6 already .

what: Rousseeuw silhouette in functional L2 space — ljmds.silhouette
title: Rousseeuw silhouette in functional L2 space — ljmds.silhouette
text: Computes Rousseeuw's silhouette (Rousseeuw, 1987) partition keywords smoothed occurrence curves \(f_j(t)\).  within distances pairwise functional \(L_2\) distances. Singleton classes contribute zero, following Rousseeuw's convention.

title: Rousseeuw silhouette in functional L2 space — ljmds.silhouette
Rousseeuw silhouette in functional L2 space — ljmds.silhouette
text: f Numeric matrix size n x p; column j smoothed occurrence curve keyword j. cl Integer vector length p cluster assignments.

title: Rousseeuw silhouette in functional L2 space — ljmds.silhouette
text: Mean silhouette width (scalar) per-keyword vector.

title: Rousseeuw silhouette in functional L2 space — ljmds.silhouette
text: Rousseeuw, P.J. (1987) Silhouettes: graphical aid interpretation validation cluster analysis. Journal Computational Applied Mathematics 20, 53–65.

title: Built-in example datasets — ljmds_data
text: Three longitudinal binary datasets ship package CSV files inst/extdata.  column 1 = time columns 2.. = 0/1 indicators.  first two derived text contain source text; third non-text demonstrates methodology applies beyond text mining.

title: Built-in example datasets — ljmds_data
text: peace_declaration.csv : 78 years (1947–2025, 1950), 95 keyword indicators English Peace Declaration Hiroshima. inaugural.csv : 59 inaugural addresses (1789–2021), 106 keyword indicators derived corpus distributed quanteda R package. eurovision.csv : 68 contest years (1956–2023), 52 country participation indicators Eurovision Song Contest. Source: Amsterdam Music Lab Mirovision dataset (MIT licence).

title: Built-in example datasets — ljmds_data

what: Plot methods for ljmds objects — plot.ljmds
title: Plot methods for ljmds objects — plot.ljmds
text: Reproduces diagnostic figures used Satoh (2026): "trajectory": cluster centroid trajectories modified MDS configuration. "dendrogram": Ward dendrogram H k coloured boxes. "cmd": time-collapsed MDS map H, points coloured class. "means": class-mean smoothed occurrence curves. "panels": keywords overlaid single panel; per class, individual smoothed occurrence curves drawn thin semi-transparent lines class mean thick opaque line colour.

title: Plot methods for ljmds objects — plot.ljmds
code: # S3 method for class 'ljmds' plot(   x,   type = c("trajectory", "dendrogram", "cmd", "means", "panels"),   class.col = grDevices::palette.colors(8, "Classic Tableau"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'ljmds.sel' plot(x, pal = grDevices::hcl.colors(64, "YlGnBu", rev = TRUE), ...)

title: Plot methods for ljmds objects — plot.ljmds
text: x object returned ljmds.pipeline(). type One "trajectory", "dendrogram", "cmd", "means", "panels". class.col Optional colour palette classes. Defaults current R palette (grDevices::palette.colors(8, "Classic Tableau")); recycled k exceeds length. ... arguments passed underlying plot calls. pal Diverging sequential palette used heatmap cells plot.ljmds.sel().  Default reversed YlGnBu sequential palette high silhouette values appear darker.

title: Plot methods for ljmds objects — plot.ljmds
text: five figure types drawn without main title caller can place caption externally (e.g. LaTeX figure environment).
